records.ety: [AS. cudu, cwudu,cwidu,cweodo, of uncertain origin; cf, G. köder bait, Icel. kviðr womb, Goth. qiþus. Cf. Quid.]

defs: That portion of food which is brought up into the mouth by ruminating animals from their first stomach, to be chewed a second time.
quotes: Whatsoever parteth the hoof, and is cloven-footed, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that shall ye eat.

defs: A portion of tobacco held in the mouth and chewed; a quid.
The first stomach of ruminating beasts.
quotes: Chewed the thrice turned cud of wrath.
